title: Cryptographic Randomness, Entropy, Keys, Salts, and Nonces · Runtime Contract: Crypto Go Key Generation Strength
summary: A service seeds math/rand with time, generates keys and reset tokens from it, retries by reusing a nonce, and cannot prove fork or startup entropy behavior. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the key generation strength increment in randomness and uniqueness contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.
Distinguish entropy sources, deterministic random bit generators, unpredictability, uniqueness, bias, and statistical appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Predict Crypto Go Key Generation Strength · Theory Crypto Go Key Generation Str…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory cryptographic engineering team handles case 6219 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to budget cryptographic randomness and nonce uniqueness across use, restart, and rollback; reject toy, obsolete, unauthenticated, self-signed-trust, downgrade, and security-by-obscurity shortcuts; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.
